key_insight: MF-PhiBE turns discrete-time transition data into a continuous-time mean-field PDE, enabling model-free control and learning on Wasserstein space without identifying the full McKean-Vlasov dynamics.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical actor-critic framework for large-population control problems where only sampled transitions are available, such as crowd dynamics, distributed energy, and networked systems.
method: Replace unknown infinitesimal drift/covariance with one-step estimators from data.
Preserve the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman generator structure on probability measures.
Combine a policy-gradient theorem for entropy-regularized randomized policies with a model-free critic.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports first-order $94t$ consistency for the optimal MF-PhiBE policy, and second-order accuracy in the linear-quadratic case using only one-step data, with experiments on LQR and a crowd-aversion benchmark.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on reliable one-step transition estimation from discrete data; noisy sampling or poor coverage may weaken the PDE approximation and policy-gradient update stability.
paper_title: Mean-Field PhiBE: Continuous-Time Mean-Field Reinforcement Learning from Discrete-Time Data
